finding: A normal framework worktree can carry repo-local `.git/config` state with `core.bare=true`, causing standard `git -C <repo> status` and Git-backed evidence commands to fail until the local config is repaired.
expectedBehavior: ATM preflight, doctor, or framework-mode status should detect a bare/worktree mismatch and report or repair the safe local setting before benchmark/evidence work begins.
evidenceOrRepro: Repaired on 2026-06-28 by adding a shared Git worktree-readiness probe used by `doctor`, `git-head-evidence`, and `framework-mode`. `doctor` now fails with `ATM_DOCTOR_GIT_WORKTREE_BARE_MISMATCH` and recommends `git config --local core.bare false`; `framework-mode status` surfaces a `git-worktree-readiness-failed` blocker; `framework-mode claim` fail-closes with `ATM_GIT_WORKTREE_READY_REQUIRED` before taking a temporary claim on a broken worktree. Regression coverage was added to `scripts/validate-git-head-evidence.ts` and `scripts/validate-framework-development-governance.ts`, and frozen-runner coverage was revalidated through `validate:root-drop-release` plus `validate:onefile-release`.
followUp: Keep the shared readiness probe as the single source of truth for worktree-backed Git diagnostics, and route future Git-backed preflight lanes through it instead of re-implementing ad hoc `git status` probes.
